name: Store Nodes in an Array
summary: Collect all node ids in order, then rewire next pointers from both ends inward (0, n-1, 1, n-2, …) — O(n) extra space.
visualizationIntent: init: show the list wired head-to-tail with left at the head and right at the tail. loop: rewire left's next to point at right (a changed link flashes), advance left inward; if left has met right stop; otherwise rewire right's next to the new left (flash) and advance right inward. The pointers close in from both ends, alternating which node gets rewired. update: after the ends meet, cap the middle node's next as the end of the list. return: traverse from the head and show the reordered value sequence.

lineExplanations.4: Start the next-pointer array (nextp[i] = the id node i points to).
lineExplanations.5: Loop over every node to wire up the original chain.
lineExplanations.6: Every node except the last points to the following node.
lineExplanations.7: Point node i at node i+1.
lineExplanations.9: The last node points at nothing (-1).
lineExplanations.10: changed marks which link just flipped (for the animation).
lineExplanations.13: Keep weaving while the two ends haven't met.
lineExplanations.14: Rewire left's next to point at right (the far end).
lineExplanations.15: Flash the link that just changed.
lineExplanations.17: If left has now reached right, the weave is done.
lineExplanations.19: Otherwise rewire right's next to point back at the new left.
lineExplanations.22: Cap the middle node so the reordered list ends cleanly.
lineExplanations.24: Start an empty list to read out the reordered values.
lineExplanations.25: Begin the read-out traversal at the head.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def reorderList(self, values): values are the node values in order.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n): loops over each node index.
syntaxExplanations.6: if i < n - 1: true for every node except the last.
syntaxExplanations.7: nextp.append(i + 1) links node i to node i+1.
syntaxExplanations.8: else: handles the last node (bare else — no step of its own).
syntaxExplanations.9: nextp.append(-1) marks the tail's next as none.
syntaxExplanations.10: changed = [] holds the id of the link that just changed.
syntaxExplanations.12: right = n - 1 starts at the tail.
syntaxExplanations.13: while left < right: weave until the ends meet.
syntaxExplanations.14: nextp[left] = right points the left node at the far end.
syntaxExplanations.15: changed = [left] flags left's link as just-changed.
syntaxExplanations.17: if left == right: has left caught up to right?
syntaxExplanations.19: nextp[right] = left points the right node back at the new left.
syntaxExplanations.20: changed = [right] flags right's link as just-changed.
syntaxExplanations.22: nextp[left] = -1 caps the middle node as the list end.
syntaxExplanations.24: order = [] will collect the reordered values.
syntaxExplanations.25: cur = 0 if n > 0 else -1 starts the read-out at the head.
syntaxExplanations.26: while cur != -1: traverse to the end.
syntaxExplanations.28: cur = nextp[cur] follows the rewired next pointer.
syntaxExplanations.29: return order hands back the reordered sequence.
